description: Memory management kernel functions for resource observability

answers: why is my container being killed

fields.name: total_vm
fields.meaning: total virtual memory of the victim

probes.use_when: containers are being killed unexpectedly. OOM kills are the kernel's last resort when memory is exhausted — the cgroup limit was hit or the node ran out of memory.
probes.not_when: you're looking for gradual memory growth — this only fires when the kernel kills a process. Use memory metrics for trending.

interpretations.pattern: oom_kill fires for a specific container repeatedly
interpretations.conclusion: container memory limit is too low, or the application has a memory leak
interpretations.action: check container memory limits. if the limit is reasonable, investigate the application for memory leaks.

interpretations.pattern: oom_kill fires for multiple different processes
interpretations.conclusion: node-level memory exhaustion — not a single container's fault
interpretations.action: check node memory pressure. too many pods scheduled on this node.
